narrative: I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimated a 44% chance that you might not pass the bar exam on your first try, which is above our threshold for admission. This prediction is based on key details from your profile, like your second-decile ranking in Year 1 grades and first-decile in Year 3—both well below the typical student's mid-range performance—along with your LSAT score of 32, undergraduate GPA of 3.10, high family income bracket, and factors such as being male, White, and planning full-time enrollment. When comparing you to other applicants, your academic deciles stand out as unusually low against the average fifth-decile marks, your LSAT is notably below the 36.7 average, and your GPA dips just under the 3.21 norm, while your high family income is stronger than most. The model's decision was shaped most strongly by your family income, which nudged the prediction toward a higher risk of bar failure despite its relative strength. Next in influence came your Year 3 decile, pushing firmly toward failure due to its bottom ranking. Your LSAT score followed closely, contributing significantly to the risk because of how much lower it was than average. Your Year 1 decile had a similar but slightly lesser pull toward failure, and your undergraduate GPA added to that concern with its modest shortfall. Other elements like race, gender, and full-time status played smaller roles, all leaning slightly toward higher risk in your case. Overall, these factors combined to elevate your predicted bar failure odds beyond what we can comfortably admit. Looking ahead, you could realistically boost your bar exam chances by focusing on strengthening your academic habits—perhaps through targeted tutoring or study strategies that mirror top performers—to offset those early low deciles in future evaluations. Retaking the LSAT for a higher score could also help if you reapply, as it was a major driver here. With dedication to these areas, students like you often turn predictions around and succeed on the bar. Keep pushing forward; your high family income suggests solid support to make these changes.
